strengths: Strong quantified impact (invoice accuracy, time reductions)
Proficient with CRM, Excel pivot tables, Asana
weaknesses: No explicit VC or startup experience
Limited mention of VIP/LP stakeholder interactions
No explicit onsite small-team NYC commitment stated

improvement_bullets: Managed dynamic calendars and scheduling for C-suite executives, prioritizing high-profile founder calls and LP engagements to ensure leadership efficiency in fast-paced, high-stakes environments.

Served as the primary point of contact for high-profile external partners and VIPs, ensuring seamless logistics and professional stakeholder communication for complex international and domestic engagements.

Collaborated within a small-team, in-office environment to execute firm-wide administrative workflows, demonstrating a 'no task too small' mindset to support daily operations and strategic project goals.
Utilized CRM systems and analytical reporting to track venture-related outreach, ensuring data accuracy and compliance with internal privacy standards for confidential document handling.

Leveraged 10+ years of executive support experience to proactively anticipate the needs of senior partners, applying the same level of discretion and precision required for supporting high-level public officials.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Executive Assistant/Office Manager opportunity with your firm. I am interested in this opportunity because of your firm’s reputation for driving innovation in the startup ecosystem; the chance to support Managing Partners in such a high-stakes, entrepreneurial setting is precisely the challenge I am looking for. What interests me about working for your firm is its commitment to backing visionary startups, which intrigues me because of my own passion for the intersection of technology and efficient business operations. I believe my expert-level calendar management and my background in complex operations would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a proactive, anticipatory mindset. In fact, at Direct Clean Solutions, I took charge of coordinating complex travel and high-priority meetings for the CEO and CFO, ensuring that all briefing materials were prepared well in advance of founder and stakeholder interactions. As a result, I reduced scheduling conflicts by 25% and maintained a 99% accuracy rate on sensitive administrative workflows, which directly enabled our leadership to focus on long-term strategy rather than logistics. Ultimately, I know that the Executive Assistant/Office Manager role at your firm would allow me to bring my high-level organizational skills to bear on the daily operations of your Managing Partners. Additionally, my role as an Executive Assistant at Apex Supply Co. enabled me to develop my vendor coordination and crisis management skills. I managed the full lifecycle of complex administrative projects, ensuring that no detail was overlooked, which led to a 50% reduction in document retrieval time. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my discretion and communication skills in working on founder calls and LP engagements at your firm. I know that my background in managing executive efficiency and my dedication to a small-team, in-office environment align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss how my experience can support your firm's continued growth. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Millieann Velez
